message:
fix: resolve SSR hydration mismatches in animated counter components

Apply consistent hydration fix pattern across all animated counter and
capability detection components to prevent React hydration mismatches.

The issue: Components using useReducedMotion() or similar browser APIs
were initializing state based on client-side values (e.g., media queries)
during SSR. Since the server cannot detect user preferences, this caused
mismatches between server-rendered HTML and client hydration.

The fix pattern:
1. Initialize state with consistent values (0/false) regardless of
   prefersReducedMotion preference
2. Use useEffect to update state after hydration when reduced motion
   is preferred
3. Wrap state updates in setTimeout(fn, 0) to defer execution until
   after React's hydration phase completes

Components updated:
- components/market-cap-drop.tsx (AnimatedCounter)
- components/x-stats-card.tsx (AnimatedNumber)
- components/stats-grid.tsx (AnimatedNumber)
- hooks/use-mobile-optimizations.ts (useDeviceCapabilities)

This ensures hydration-safe initialization while still respecting user
accessibility preferences for reduced motion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
